prompts.abstract: The object is a rectangular prism with a protruding rectangular section on one side and a cut-out on the opposite side. It has a flat top and bottom surfaces.
prompts.description: The object is a rectangular prism with a protruding rectangular section on one side and a cut-out on the opposite side. It has a flat top and bottom surfaces.
prompts.beginner: The design features a series of connected shapes, including a rectangular bar, a cylinder with a rectangular top, a rectangular prism with a cylindrical section, a cylinder with a rectangular top and circular base, and a cylinder with a rectangular top and conical bottom. Each part adds unique dimensions to create a complex, multi-sectioned structure.
prompts.intermediate: The design consists of five interconnected parts. The first part is a rectangular bar with a length of 0.6 units, a width of 0.1 units, and a height of 0.06 units. The second part is a cylinder with a rectangular top, adding 0.6 units in length, 0.3794 units in width, and 0.1 units in height to the existing body. The third part is a rectangular prism with a cylindrical section, contributing 0.4075 units in length, 0.1306 units in width, and 0.1 units in height. The fourth part is a cylinder with a rectangular top and a circular base, extending the body by 0.6 units in length, 0.14 units in width, and 0.1 units in height. The fifth part is a cylinder with a rectangular top and a conical bottom, adding 0.2578 units in length, 0.1306 units in width, and 0.06 units in height. These parts are assembled to form a complex, multi-sectioned solid body.
prompts.expert:
Create a rectangular bar by first setting up a new coordinate system with Euler angles of 0.0, 0.0, and -90.0 degrees, and a translation vector of 0.0, 0.1, and 0.65 units. Next, draw a two-dimensional sketch on a new face, starting with a closed loop defined by four lines: the first line from (0.0, 0.0) to (0.6, 0.0), the second from (0.6, 0.0) to (0.6, 0.1), the third from (0.6, 0.1) to (0.0, 0.1), and the fourth from (0.0, 0.1) back to (0.0, 0.0). Scale this sketch by a factor of 0.6. Transform the scaled sketch into three dimensions by rotating it according to the Euler angles and translating it using the specified vector. Finally, extrude the sketch along the normal direction by 0.06 units to create a solid body with final dimensions of 0.6 units in length, 0.1 units in width, and 0.06 units in height.

For the second part, construct a cylinder with a rectangular top by creating another new coordinate system with the same Euler angles and a translation vector of 0.0, 0.1, and 0.2706 units. Draw a two-dimensional sketch on a new face, forming a closed loop with five lines: the first from (0.0, 0.0) to (0.1925, 0.0), the second from (0.1925, 0.0) to (0.6, 0.0), the third from (0.6, 0.0) to (0.6, 0.3794), the fourth from (0.6, 0.3794) to (0.0, 0.3794), and the fifth from (0.0, 0.3794) back to (0.0, 0.0). Scale this sketch by a factor of 0.6, transform it into three dimensions, and extrude it along the normal direction by 0.1 units. This will add a new section to the existing body with final dimensions of 0.6 units in length, 0.3794 units in width, and 0.1 units in height.

In the third part, create a rectangular prism with a cylindrical section by setting up a new coordinate system with the same Euler angles and a translation vector of 0.1925, 0.1, and 0.14 units. Draw a two-dimensional sketch on a new face, forming a closed loop with four lines and one arc: the first line from (0.0, 0.0) to (0.4075, 0.0), the second from (0.4075, 0.0) to (0.4075, 0.125), the third from (0.4075, 0.125) to (0.4075, 0.1306), the fourth from (0.4075, 0.1306) to (0.0, 0.1306), and the arc from (0.0, 0.1306) through (0.0653, 0.0653) back to (0.0, 0.0). Scale this sketch by a factor of 0.4075, transform it into three dimensions, and extrude it along the normal direction by 0.1 units. This will add a new section to the existing body with final dimensions of 0.4075 units in length, 0.1306 units in width, and 0.1 units in height.

For the fourth part, construct a cylinder with a rectangular top and circular base by setting up a new coordinate system with the same Euler angles and a translation vector of 0.0, 0.1, and 0.0 units. Draw a two-dimensional sketch on a new face, forming a closed loop with five lines: the first from (0.0, 0.0) to (0.6, 0.0), the second from (0.6, 0.0) to (0.6, 0.14), the third from (0.6, 0.14) to (0.1925, 0.14), the fourth from (0.1925, 0.14) to (0.0, 0.14), and the fifth from (0.0, 0.14) back to (0.0, 0.0). Scale this sketch by a factor of 0.6, transform it into three dimensions, and extrude it along the normal direction by 0.1 units. This will add a new section to the existing body with final dimensions of 0.6 units in length, 0.14 units in width, and 0.1 units in height.

Finally, for the fifth part, create a cylinder with a rectangular top and conical bottom by setting up a new coordinate system with the same Euler angles and a translation vector of 0.0, 0.1, and 0.14 units. Draw a two-dimensional sketch on a new face, forming a closed loop with three lines and one arc: the first line from (0.0, 0.0) to (0.1925, 0.0), the arc from (0.1925, 0.0) through (0.2578, 0.0653) to (0.1925, 0.1306), the second line from (0.1925, 0.1306) to (0.0, 0.1306), and the third line from (0.0, 0.1306) back to (0.0, 0.0). Scale this sketch by a factor of 0.2578, transform it into three dimensions, and extrude it along the normal direction by 0.06 units. This will add a new section to the existing body with final dimensions of 0.2578 units in length, 0.1306 units in width, and 0.06 units in height.

prompts.nli_data:
<part_1>
### Construct a Rectangular Bar
#### Step 1: Create a New Coordinate System
- Set Euler Angles to 0.0, 0.0, -90.0.
- Set Translation Vector to 0.0, 0.1, 0.65.

#### Step 2: Draw a 2D Sketch
- Create a new face.
- Draw the first loop:
  - Line 1: Start at (0.0, 0.0), end at (0.6, 0.0).
  - Line 2: Start at (0.6, 0.0), end at (0.6, 0.1).
  - Line 3: Start at (0.6, 0.1), end at (0.0, 0.1).
  - Line 4: Start at (0.0, 0.1), end at (0.0, 0.0).

#### Step 3: Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a sketch scale of 0.6.

#### Step 4: Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate using the Euler Angles.
- Translate using the Translation Vector.

#### Step 5: Extrude the 2D Sketch
- Extrude the sketch towards the normal by 0.06 units.
- Do not extrude in the opposite direction.
- This will create a new solid body.

#### Final Dimensions
- Length: 0.6
- Width: 0.1
- Height: 0.06
</part_1>

<part_2>
### Construct a Cylinder with a Rectangular Top
#### Step 1: Create a New Coordinate System
- Set Euler Angles to 0.0, 0.0, -90.0.
- Set Translation Vector to 0.0, 0.1, 0.2706.

#### Step 2: Draw a 2D Sketch
- Create a new face.
- Draw the first loop:
  - Line 1: Start at (0.0, 0.0), end at (0.1925, 0.0).
  - Line 2: Start at (0.1925, 0.0), end at (0.6, 0.0).
  - Line 3: Start at (0.6, 0.0), end at (0.6, 0.3794).
  - Line 4: Start at (0.6, 0.3794), end at (0.0, 0.3794).
  - Line 5: Start at (0.0, 0.3794), end at (0.0, 0.0).

#### Step 3: Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a sketch scale of 0.6.

#### Step 4: Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate using the Euler Angles.
- Translate using the Translation Vector.

#### Step 5: Extrude the 2D Sketch
- Extrude the sketch towards the normal by 0.1 units.
- Do not extrude in the opposite direction.
- Add this part to the existing body.

#### Final Dimensions
- Length: 0.6
- Width: 0.3794
- Height: 0.1
</part_2>

<part_3>

  - Arc 1: Start at (0.0, 0.1306), mid at (0.0653, 0.0653), end at (0.0, 0.0).

- Apply a sketch scale of 0.4075.

#### Step 4: Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate using the Euler Angles.
